input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

samples.output: <think>Three men in military garrison caps appear against a dark background. The central figure wears a dark cap with light piping and an insignia. To his left, a man in a light-colored cap looks forward. A third man in a similar cap is partially visible on the right edge. The framing shifts to reveal their faces.</think><answer></answer>

queries.question: Describe the sequence of visual events regarding the personnel and aircraft shown throughout the clip.

answers.text: The video begins with three men in military caps, followed by four men in leather flight jackets appearing shortly after. A person is then seen climbing a metal ladder structure. Subsequently, a twin-engine bomber aircraft is displayed with a text overlay describing the mission. Finally, the scene shifts to show several individuals standing near the aircraft on the flight deck, focusing on the engine, propeller, and landing gear details.

Following the initial deck scenes, the camera highlights the aircraft's engine and propeller assembly with personnel nearby. A text overlay then appears, identifying the mission as striking targets in and around Tokyo, framed by a low-angle view of the landing gear. Finally, the wheels lift off the gray flight deck as the bomber ascends, revealing the tail number "283" before the mission text disappears from the screen.

As the bomber ascends, its full profile including the star insignia becomes visible against the overcast sky with landing gear still extended. The aircraft continues to fly away, shrinking in size above the choppy ocean. In the foreground, dark silhouettes of crew members and ship-mounted gun turrets appear on the right, observing the plane's departure from the flight deck.

The video concludes with an indoor interview featuring an elderly, bald man in a dark collared shirt seated before a wooden shelf. A static text overlay identifies him as Lt. Richard E. Cole, Co-Pilot of Crew No. 1. He appears to be speaking, with his mouth opening slightly while maintaining a gaze directed off-camera to the left, shifting focus entirely from the previous outdoor flight deck sequences.

answers.text: The video begins with three men in military caps, followed by four men in leather flight jackets appearing shortly after. A person is then seen climbing a metal ladder structure. Subsequently, a twin-engine bomber aircraft is displayed with a text overlay describing the mission. Finally, the scene shifts to show several individuals standing near the aircraft on the flight deck, focusing on the engine, propeller, and landing gear details.

Following the initial deck scenes, the camera highlights the aircraft's engine and propeller assembly with personnel nearby. A text overlay then appears, identifying the mission as striking targets in and around Tokyo, framed by a low-angle view of the landing gear. Finally, the wheels lift off the gray flight deck as the bomber ascends, revealing the tail number "283" before the mission text disappears from the screen.

As the bomber ascends, its full profile including the star insignia becomes visible against the overcast sky with landing gear still extended. The aircraft continues to fly away, shrinking in size above the choppy ocean. In the foreground, dark silhouettes of crew members and ship-mounted gun turrets appear on the right, observing the plane's departure from the flight deck.

The video concludes with an indoor interview featuring an elderly, bald man in a dark collared shirt seated before a wooden shelf. A static text overlay identifies him as Lt. Richard E. Cole, Co-Pilot of Crew No. 1. He appears to be speaking, with his mouth opening slightly while maintaining a gaze directed off-camera to the left, shifting focus entirely from the previous outdoor flight deck sequences.

queries.question: Describe the sequence of visual events regarding the personnel and aircraft shown throughout the clip.

answers.text: The video begins with three men in military caps, followed by four men in leather flight jackets appearing shortly after. A person is then seen climbing a metal ladder structure. Subsequently, a twin-engine bomber aircraft is displayed with a text overlay describing the mission. Finally, the scene shifts to show several individuals standing near the aircraft on the flight deck, focusing on the engine, propeller, and landing gear details.

Following the initial deck scenes, the camera highlights the aircraft's engine and propeller assembly with personnel nearby. A text overlay then appears, identifying the mission as striking targets in and around Tokyo, framed by a low-angle view of the landing gear. Finally, the wheels lift off the gray flight deck as the bomber ascends, revealing the tail number "283" before the mission text disappears from the screen.

As the bomber ascends, its full profile including the star insignia becomes visible against the overcast sky with landing gear still extended. The aircraft continues to fly away, shrinking in size above the choppy ocean. In the foreground, dark silhouettes of crew members and ship-mounted gun turrets appear on the right, observing the plane's departure from the flight deck.

The video concludes with an indoor interview featuring an elderly, bald man in a dark collared shirt seated before a wooden shelf. A static text overlay identifies him as Lt. Richard E. Cole, Co-Pilot of Crew No. 1. He appears to be speaking, with his mouth opening slightly while maintaining a gaze directed off-camera to the left, shifting focus entirely from the previous outdoor flight deck sequences.

The sequence concludes with the twin-engine bomber taxiing forward on the flight deck, bringing its nose and cockpit extremely close to the camera lens. The perspective shifts to highlight the left wing extending into the foreground and the top of the fuselage, with the pilot visible inside. The aircraft continues moving leftward, passing the camera to reveal the mid-fuselage and tail section, while blurred propellers indicate rotation against the gray ocean backdrop.

queries.question: Describe the sequence of visual events regarding the personnel and aircraft shown throughout the clip.

answers.text: The video begins with three men in military caps, followed by four men in leather flight jackets appearing shortly after. A person is then seen climbing a metal ladder structure. Subsequently, a twin-engine bomber aircraft is displayed with a text overlay describing the mission. Finally, the scene shifts to show several individuals standing near the aircraft on the flight deck, focusing on the engine, propeller, and landing gear details.

Following the initial deck scenes, the camera highlights the aircraft's engine and propeller assembly with personnel nearby. A text overlay then appears, identifying the mission as striking targets in and around Tokyo, framed by a low-angle view of the landing gear. Finally, the wheels lift off the gray flight deck as the bomber ascends, revealing the tail number "283" before the mission text disappears from the screen.

As the bomber ascends, its full profile including the star insignia becomes visible against the overcast sky with landing gear still extended. The aircraft continues to fly away, shrinking in size above the choppy ocean. In the foreground, dark silhouettes of crew members and ship-mounted gun turrets appear on the right, observing the plane's departure from the flight deck.

The video concludes with an indoor interview featuring an elderly, bald man in a dark collared shirt seated before a wooden shelf. A static text overlay identifies him as Lt. Richard E. Cole, Co-Pilot of Crew No. 1. He appears to be speaking, with his mouth opening slightly while maintaining a gaze directed off-camera to the left, shifting focus entirely from the previous outdoor flight deck sequences.

The sequence concludes with the twin-engine bomber taxiing forward on the flight deck, bringing its nose and cockpit extremely close to the camera lens. The perspective shifts to highlight the left wing extending into the foreground and the top of the fuselage, with the pilot visible inside. The aircraft continues moving leftward, passing the camera to reveal the mid-fuselage and tail section, while blurred propellers indicate rotation against the gray ocean backdrop.

queries.question: Describe the sequence of visual events regarding the personnel and aircraft shown throughout the clip.

answers.text: The video begins with three men in military caps, followed by four men in leather flight jackets appearing shortly after. A person is then seen climbing a metal ladder structure. Subsequently, a twin-engine bomber aircraft is displayed with a text overlay describing the mission. Finally, the scene shifts to show several individuals standing near the aircraft on the flight deck, focusing on the engine, propeller, and landing gear details.

Following the initial deck scenes, the camera highlights the aircraft's engine and propeller assembly with personnel nearby. A text overlay then appears, identifying the mission as striking targets in and around Tokyo, framed by a low-angle view of the landing gear. Finally, the wheels lift off the gray flight deck as the bomber ascends, revealing the tail number "283" before the mission text disappears from the screen.

As the bomber ascends, its full profile including the star insignia becomes visible against the overcast sky with landing gear still extended. The aircraft continues to fly away, shrinking in size above the choppy ocean. In the foreground, dark silhouettes of crew members and ship-mounted gun turrets appear on the right, observing the plane's departure from the flight deck.

The video concludes with an indoor interview featuring an elderly, bald man in a dark collared shirt seated before a wooden shelf. A static text overlay identifies him as Lt. Richard E. Cole, Co-Pilot of Crew No. 1. He appears to be speaking, with his mouth opening slightly while maintaining a gaze directed off-camera to the left, shifting focus entirely from the previous outdoor flight deck sequences.

The sequence concludes with the twin-engine bomber taxiing forward on the flight deck, bringing its nose and cockpit extremely close to the camera lens. The perspective shifts to highlight the left wing extending into the foreground and the top of the fuselage, with the pilot visible inside. The aircraft continues moving leftward, passing the camera to reveal the mid-fuselage and tail section, while blurred propellers indicate rotation against the gray ocean backdrop.

queries.question: Describe the sequence of visual events regarding the personnel and aircraft shown throughout the clip.

answers.text: The video begins with three men in military caps, followed by four men in leather flight jackets appearing shortly after. A person is then seen climbing a metal ladder structure. Subsequently, a twin-engine bomber aircraft is displayed with a text overlay describing the mission. Finally, the scene shifts to show several individuals standing near the aircraft on the flight deck, focusing on the engine, propeller, and landing gear details.

Following the initial deck scenes, the camera highlights the aircraft's engine and propeller assembly with personnel nearby. A text overlay then appears, identifying the mission as striking targets in and around Tokyo, framed by a low-angle view of the landing gear. Finally, the wheels lift off the gray flight deck as the bomber ascends, revealing the tail number "283" before the mission text disappears from the screen.

As the bomber ascends, its full profile including the star insignia becomes visible against the overcast sky with landing gear still extended. The aircraft continues to fly away, shrinking in size above the choppy ocean. In the foreground, dark silhouettes of crew members and ship-mounted gun turrets appear on the right, observing the plane's departure from the flight deck.

The video concludes with an indoor interview featuring an elderly, bald man in a dark collared shirt seated before a wooden shelf. A static text overlay identifies him as Lt. Richard E. Cole, Co-Pilot of Crew No. 1. He appears to be speaking, with his mouth opening slightly while maintaining a gaze directed off-camera to the left, shifting focus entirely from the previous outdoor flight deck sequences.

The sequence concludes with the twin-engine bomber taxiing forward on the flight deck, bringing its nose and cockpit extremely close to the camera lens. The perspective shifts to highlight the left wing extending into the foreground and the top of the fuselage, with the pilot visible inside. The aircraft continues moving leftward, passing the camera to reveal the mid-fuselage and tail section, while blurred propellers indicate rotation against the gray ocean backdrop.

samples.output: <think>The elderly bald man in a dark collared shirt continues speaking, his mouth moving. He blinks his eyes closed briefly. The background remains static, featuring a wooden shelf with a red plate and white rabbit figurine on the left side of the frame.</think><answer></answer>

queries.question: Describe the sequence of visual events regarding the personnel and aircraft shown throughout the clip.

answers.text: The video begins with three men in military caps, followed by four men in leather flight jackets appearing shortly after. A person is then seen climbing a metal ladder structure. Subsequently, a twin-engine bomber aircraft is displayed with a text overlay describing the mission. Finally, the scene shifts to show several individuals standing near the aircraft on the flight deck, focusing on the engine, propeller, and landing gear details.

Following the initial deck scenes, the camera highlights the aircraft's engine and propeller assembly with personnel nearby. A text overlay then appears, identifying the mission as striking targets in and around Tokyo, framed by a low-angle view of the landing gear. Finally, the wheels lift off the gray flight deck as the bomber ascends, revealing the tail number "283" before the mission text disappears from the screen.

As the bomber ascends, its full profile including the star insignia becomes visible against the overcast sky with landing gear still extended. The aircraft continues to fly away, shrinking in size above the choppy ocean. In the foreground, dark silhouettes of crew members and ship-mounted gun turrets appear on the right, observing the plane's departure from the flight deck.

The video concludes with an indoor interview featuring an elderly, bald man in a dark collared shirt seated before a wooden shelf. A static text overlay identifies him as Lt. Richard E. Cole, Co-Pilot of Crew No. 1. He appears to be speaking, with his mouth opening slightly while maintaining a gaze directed off-camera to the left, shifting focus entirely from the previous outdoor flight deck sequences.

The sequence concludes with the twin-engine bomber taxiing forward on the flight deck, bringing its nose and cockpit extremely close to the camera lens. The perspective shifts to highlight the left wing extending into the foreground and the top of the fuselage, with the pilot visible inside. The aircraft continues moving leftward, passing the camera to reveal the mid-fuselage and tail section, while blurred propellers indicate rotation against the gray ocean backdrop.

answers.text: The video begins with three men in military caps, followed by four men in leather flight jackets appearing shortly after. A person is then seen climbing a metal ladder structure. Subsequently, a twin-engine bomber aircraft is displayed with a text overlay describing the mission. Finally, the scene shifts to show several individuals standing near the aircraft on the flight deck, focusing on the engine, propeller, and landing gear details.

Following the initial deck scenes, the camera highlights the aircraft's engine and propeller assembly with personnel nearby. A text overlay then appears, identifying the mission as striking targets in and around Tokyo, framed by a low-angle view of the landing gear. Finally, the wheels lift off the gray flight deck as the bomber ascends, revealing the tail number "283" before the mission text disappears from the screen.

As the bomber ascends, its full profile including the star insignia becomes visible against the overcast sky with landing gear still extended. The aircraft continues to fly away, shrinking in size above the choppy ocean. In the foreground, dark silhouettes of crew members and ship-mounted gun turrets appear on the right, observing the plane's departure from the flight deck.

The video concludes with an indoor interview featuring an elderly, bald man in a dark collared shirt seated before a wooden shelf. A static text overlay identifies him as Lt. Richard E. Cole, Co-Pilot of Crew No. 1. He appears to be speaking, with his mouth opening slightly while maintaining a gaze directed off-camera to the left, shifting focus entirely from the previous outdoor flight deck sequences.

The sequence concludes with the twin-engine bomber taxiing forward on the flight deck, bringing its nose and cockpit extremely close to the camera lens. The perspective shifts to highlight the left wing extending into the foreground and the top of the fuselage, with the pilot visible inside. The aircraft continues moving leftward, passing the camera to reveal the mid-fuselage and tail section, while blurred propellers indicate rotation against the gray ocean backdrop.

queries.question: Describe the sequence of visual events regarding the personnel and aircraft shown throughout the clip.

answers.text: The video begins with three men in military caps, followed by four men in leather flight jackets appearing shortly after. A person is then seen climbing a metal ladder structure. Subsequently, a twin-engine bomber aircraft is displayed with a text overlay describing the mission. Finally, the scene shifts to show several individuals standing near the aircraft on the flight deck, focusing on the engine, propeller, and landing gear details.

Following the initial deck scenes, the camera highlights the aircraft's engine and propeller assembly with personnel nearby. A text overlay then appears, identifying the mission as striking targets in and around Tokyo, framed by a low-angle view of the landing gear. Finally, the wheels lift off the gray flight deck as the bomber ascends, revealing the tail number "283" before the mission text disappears from the screen.

As the bomber ascends, its full profile including the star insignia becomes visible against the overcast sky with landing gear still extended. The aircraft continues to fly away, shrinking in size above the choppy ocean. In the foreground, dark silhouettes of crew members and ship-mounted gun turrets appear on the right, observing the plane's departure from the flight deck.

The video concludes with an indoor interview featuring an elderly, bald man in a dark collared shirt seated before a wooden shelf. A static text overlay identifies him as Lt. Richard E. Cole, Co-Pilot of Crew No. 1. He appears to be speaking, with his mouth opening slightly while maintaining a gaze directed off-camera to the left, shifting focus entirely from the previous outdoor flight deck sequences.

The sequence concludes with the twin-engine bomber taxiing forward on the flight deck, bringing its nose and cockpit extremely close to the camera lens. The perspective shifts to highlight the left wing extending into the foreground and the top of the fuselage, with the pilot visible inside. The aircraft continues moving leftward, passing the camera to reveal the mid-fuselage and tail section, while blurred propellers indicate rotation against the gray ocean backdrop.

The footage transitions via dissolve to an elderly man speaking indoors, then cuts to black-and-white archival scenes of uniformed figures running with trees down a city street. A high-angle view establishes a dense urban skyline before shifting to a low-angle shot of the bomber flying overhead with landing gear extended. Finally, the aircraft maintains a steady, low-altitude trajectory against a hazy sky, moving leftward over a blurry landscape of hills or buildings as its propellers blur.

queries.question: Which city is identified as the target in the mission status text overlay? A. Tokyo B. Hornet C. Raid D. Mitchell

answers.text: A

queries.question: Describe the sequence of visual events regarding the personnel and aircraft shown throughout the clip.

answers.text: The video begins with three men in military caps, followed by four men in leather flight jackets appearing shortly after. A person is then seen climbing a metal ladder structure. Subsequently, a twin-engine bomber aircraft is displayed with a text overlay describing the mission. Finally, the scene shifts to show several individuals standing near the aircraft on the flight deck, focusing on the engine, propeller, and landing gear details.

Following the initial deck scenes, the camera highlights the aircraft's engine and propeller assembly with personnel nearby. A text overlay then appears, identifying the mission as striking targets in and around Tokyo, framed by a low-angle view of the landing gear. Finally, the wheels lift off the gray flight deck as the bomber ascends, revealing the tail number "283" before the mission text disappears from the screen.

As the bomber ascends, its full profile including the star insignia becomes visible against the overcast sky with landing gear still extended. The aircraft continues to fly away, shrinking in size above the choppy ocean. In the foreground, dark silhouettes of crew members and ship-mounted gun turrets appear on the right, observing the plane's departure from the flight deck.

The video concludes with an indoor interview featuring an elderly, bald man in a dark collared shirt seated before a wooden shelf. A static text overlay identifies him as Lt. Richard E. Cole, Co-Pilot of Crew No. 1. He appears to be speaking, with his mouth opening slightly while maintaining a gaze directed off-camera to the left, shifting focus entirely from the previous outdoor flight deck sequences.

The sequence concludes with the twin-engine bomber taxiing forward on the flight deck, bringing its nose and cockpit extremely close to the camera lens. The perspective shifts to highlight the left wing extending into the foreground and the top of the fuselage, with the pilot visible inside. The aircraft continues moving leftward, passing the camera to reveal the mid-fuselage and tail section, while blurred propellers indicate rotation against the gray ocean backdrop.

The footage transitions via dissolve to an elderly man speaking indoors, then cuts to black-and-white archival scenes of uniformed figures running with trees down a city street. A high-angle view establishes a dense urban skyline before shifting to a low-angle shot of the bomber flying overhead with landing gear extended. Finally, the aircraft maintains a steady, low-altitude trajectory against a hazy sky, moving leftward over a blurry landscape of hills or buildings as its propellers blur.

queries.question: Which city is identified as the target in the mission status text overlay? A. Tokyo B. Hornet C. Raid D. Mitchell

answers.text: A

queries.question: Describe the sequence of visual events regarding the personnel and aircraft shown throughout the clip.

answers.text: The video begins with three men in military caps, followed by four men in leather flight jackets appearing shortly after. A person is then seen climbing a metal ladder structure. Subsequently, a twin-engine bomber aircraft is displayed with a text overlay describing the mission. Finally, the scene shifts to show several individuals standing near the aircraft on the flight deck, focusing on the engine, propeller, and landing gear details.

Following the initial deck scenes, the camera highlights the aircraft's engine and propeller assembly with personnel nearby. A text overlay then appears, identifying the mission as striking targets in and around Tokyo, framed by a low-angle view of the landing gear. Finally, the wheels lift off the gray flight deck as the bomber ascends, revealing the tail number "283" before the mission text disappears from the screen.

As the bomber ascends, its full profile including the star insignia becomes visible against the overcast sky with landing gear still extended. The aircraft continues to fly away, shrinking in size above the choppy ocean. In the foreground, dark silhouettes of crew members and ship-mounted gun turrets appear on the right, observing the plane's departure from the flight deck.

The video concludes with an indoor interview featuring an elderly, bald man in a dark collared shirt seated before a wooden shelf. A static text overlay identifies him as Lt. Richard E. Cole, Co-Pilot of Crew No. 1. He appears to be speaking, with his mouth opening slightly while maintaining a gaze directed off-camera to the left, shifting focus entirely from the previous outdoor flight deck sequences.

The sequence concludes with the twin-engine bomber taxiing forward on the flight deck, bringing its nose and cockpit extremely close to the camera lens. The perspective shifts to highlight the left wing extending into the foreground and the top of the fuselage, with the pilot visible inside. The aircraft continues moving leftward, passing the camera to reveal the mid-fuselage and tail section, while blurred propellers indicate rotation against the gray ocean backdrop.

The footage transitions via dissolve to an elderly man speaking indoors, then cuts to black-and-white archival scenes of uniformed figures running with trees down a city street. A high-angle view establishes a dense urban skyline before shifting to a low-angle shot of the bomber flying overhead with landing gear extended. Finally, the aircraft maintains a steady, low-altitude trajectory against a hazy sky, moving leftward over a blurry landscape of hills or buildings as its propellers blur.

queries.question: Describe the sequence of visual events regarding the personnel and aircraft shown throughout the clip.

answers.text: The video begins with three men in military caps, followed by four men in leather flight jackets appearing shortly after. A person is then seen climbing a metal ladder structure. Subsequently, a twin-engine bomber aircraft is displayed with a text overlay describing the mission. Finally, the scene shifts to show several individuals standing near the aircraft on the flight deck, focusing on the engine, propeller, and landing gear details.

Following the initial deck scenes, the camera highlights the aircraft's engine and propeller assembly with personnel nearby. A text overlay then appears, identifying the mission as striking targets in and around Tokyo, framed by a low-angle view of the landing gear. Finally, the wheels lift off the gray flight deck as the bomber ascends, revealing the tail number "283" before the mission text disappears from the screen.

As the bomber ascends, its full profile including the star insignia becomes visible against the overcast sky with landing gear still extended. The aircraft continues to fly away, shrinking in size above the choppy ocean. In the foreground, dark silhouettes of crew members and ship-mounted gun turrets appear on the right, observing the plane's departure from the flight deck.

The video concludes with an indoor interview featuring an elderly, bald man in a dark collared shirt seated before a wooden shelf. A static text overlay identifies him as Lt. Richard E. Cole, Co-Pilot of Crew No. 1. He appears to be speaking, with his mouth opening slightly while maintaining a gaze directed off-camera to the left, shifting focus entirely from the previous outdoor flight deck sequences.

The sequence concludes with the twin-engine bomber taxiing forward on the flight deck, bringing its nose and cockpit extremely close to the camera lens. The perspective shifts to highlight the left wing extending into the foreground and the top of the fuselage, with the pilot visible inside. The aircraft continues moving leftward, passing the camera to reveal the mid-fuselage and tail section, while blurred propellers indicate rotation against the gray ocean backdrop.

The footage transitions via dissolve to an elderly man speaking indoors, then cuts to black-and-white archival scenes of uniformed figures running with trees down a city street. A high-angle view establishes a dense urban skyline before shifting to a low-angle shot of the bomber flying overhead with landing gear extended. Finally, the aircraft maintains a steady, low-altitude trajectory against a hazy sky, moving leftward over a blurry landscape of hills or buildings as its propellers blur.

queries.question: Which city is identified as the target in the mission status text overlay? A. Tokyo B. Hornet C. Raid D. Mitchell

answers.text: A

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

The man on the right is fully visible. All three face forward, with the central figure looking directly at the lens.

[4-6] The camera zooms out to reveal two additional men on the left, totaling five. They wear dark garrison caps and leather flight jackets; the far-left man displays a circular patch with a bird emblem, while the man beside him has a diamond-shaped patch. The central figure is now fully visible in a leather jacket with a matching bird patch.

[6-8] The video cuts to a grainy, high-contrast interior scene where a figure in light clothing climbs a ladder or structure on the right. Another individual on the left reaches toward the climber. This dynamic action replaces the static group portrait of the five men in leather jackets seen previously.

[8-10] The scene cuts to an exterior airfield setting. A uniformed figure with a peaked cap stands in the foreground near a large aircraft propeller on the right. In the background, a group of personnel in dark uniforms walks across a tarmac under a bright, overcast sky, replacing the previous interior setting.

[10-12] White text reading "Doolittle Raid" and "Commander: Lt. Col. Jimmy Do" overlays a view of a large bomber aircraft's nose. The plane features a glass cockpit and dark fuselage. Several figures in dark uniforms stand on the tarmac near the landing gear and propellers.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text: [0-6] The video opens with a close-up of three men in military garrison caps against a dark background. The camera zooms out to reveal a total of five men wearing leather flight jackets. The central figure wears a dark cap with light piping and a jacket with a circular bird emblem patch. To his left, a man wears a jacket with a diamond-shaped patch, while the man on the far left displays a similar bird emblem. [6-10] The scene cuts to a grainy, high-contrast interior where a figure in light clothing climbs a ladder structure. This transitions to an exterior airfield setting where a uniformed officer with a peaked cap stands near a large aircraft propeller, with a group of personnel walking on the tarmac in the background. [10-14] White text overlays appear over the nose of a large bomber aircraft, reading "Doolittle Raid" and "Commander: Lt. Col. Jimmy Do." The text updates to include the full name "Commander: Lt. Col. Jimmy Doolittle" and the date "April 18, 1942" as the view shifts to the aircraft's wing and engine area. [14-18] The camera focuses on a large aircraft engine and propeller assembly. Several individuals in dark uniforms stand beneath the

[28-32] A low-angle perspective captures black landing gear wheels on a gray flight deck with visible markings while the white text "MISSION: Strike Targets in and Around Tokyo" is displayed at the bottom. The wheels lift off the deck surface, the text vanishes, and the tail section marked "283" becomes clearly visible.
[32-36] The twin-engine bomber ascends with extended landing gear, showing a star insignia against a bright, overcast sky. As the aircraft shrinks flying over the gray, choppy ocean, dark silhouettes of crew members and ship-mounted gun turrets appear in the foreground right.
[36-42] Historical footage cuts to a modern color interview featuring an elderly, bald man with light skin wearing a dark collared shirt, his mouth partially open. He sits indoors before a wooden shelf holding books and a decorative plate. White text appears reading "Lt. Richard E. Cole," then expands to include "Co-Pilot, Crew No. 1" as he looks slightly off-camera.

[42-46] An elderly, bald man in a dark collared shirt sits indoors before a wooden shelf, speaking while the white text "Lt. Richard E. Cole" and "Co-Pilot, Crew No. 1" remains static in the lower left. A dissolve transition begins, blending his translucent face with archival footage. [46-50] The view resolves to a high-angle shot of an aircraft carrier flight deck where multiple twin-engine bombers are parked in rows, secured by white braces. Personnel in dark uniforms move across the gray deck near the railing, with choppy ocean waves in the background. The camera zooms in on the dark aircraft bodies, highlighting white star insignias. [50-56] The scene cuts to a medium shot of a single twin-engine bomber taxiing forward-left on the wet deck. Its landing gear rolls along the surface as propellers blur with motion. The aircraft advances toward the camera, bringing the nose and cockpit into focus, where a pilot is faintly visible behind the glass.

[56-60] A twin-engine bomber taxis on a flight deck, bringing its nose and cockpit close to the lens before the view shifts to the left wing and fuselage top with the pilot visible. The aircraft continues leftward past the camera, revealing the mid-fuselage, wing, and tail section with blurred propellers against the ocean.
[60-62] A dissolve transition blends the bomber's tail with a modern color interview, superimposing an elderly man's face as the black-and-white footage fades to show him speaking with eyes closed.
[62-66] The scene cuts to black-and-white archival footage of uniformed figures running down a city street carrying large leafy trees, followed by a high-angle view of a dense city skyline with rectangular buildings.
[66-70] The footage cuts to a low-angle shot of a dark twin-engine bomber flying overhead with landing gear extended against a bright sky, then flying low over a blurry landscape with propellers blurring as it approaches ground level.

[70-74] Grainy, black-and-white footage maintains a tight, frontal close-up on a bomber's nose section as it flies forward, the rounded glass cockpit dominating the center flanked by dark wings against a plain sky. [74-82] The view shifts to a light-colored cylindrical bomb falling vertically through billowing white clouds and a gray atmosphere; the object descends from the upper frame toward the bottom, appearing smaller amidst scattered specks. [82-84] Finally, a dark twin-engine bomber is viewed from the rear, displaying two vertical tail stabilizers and wing-mounted engines as it flies away over a thick layer of white clouds.

The plane advances through the frame, revealing its upper fuselage, engines, and cockpit structure against the dark background below.
